0x32c9802fd247d6049bd22b038b245d3d92be89.question: Will Mariah Carey have a #1 song on the Billboard Hot 100 in 2026?

analysis.question: Will Mariah Carey have a #1 song on the Billboard Hot 100 in 2026?
analysis.fullAnalysis:
1. CONTEXT ANALYSIS  
The market asks whether any song credited to Mariah Carey as a primary artist will reach #1 on any weekly Billboard Hot 100 chart whose data collection period falls entirely within January 1–December 31, 2026. A “Yes” requires at least one #1 placement in one of the 52 charts from the Week of January 17, 2026 through the Week of January 9, 2027 (data Dec 25–31, 2026).

2. EVIDENCE EVALUATION  
Bullish evidence  
• Historic Holiday Dominance: “All I Want for Christmas Is You” has now logged 20 weeks at #1 on the Hot 100 (Dec 2025) across multiple holiday seasons, implying it reliably reclaims the top spot each December.  
• Recurring Pattern: If it continues the 3–5-week holiday run it’s posted annually since 2019, it will almost certainly be #1 in late Dec 2026 (chart Week of Jan 9, 2027).  
• Industry Chatter & Legacy: Rumors and anniversaries suggest possible new releases, but those are secondary given the established holiday single.

Bearish/speculative counterpoints  
• Seasonal Drop-off Risk: Post-Christmas streaming could underperform, but historically that hasn’t prevented at least one week at #1 in the final holiday chart.  
• No confirmed new single: New music could fail, but not needed for resolution.

Source credibility  
• Billboard (Katie Cao, Gary Trust): high  
• CassWorld: moderate for career summary  
• ad-hoc-news: lower but consistent

3. MARKET EFFICIENCY ANALYSIS  
An 85% price is plausibly the consensus that Carey will hit #1, largely via her Christmas classic. Low volume suggests a few traders set the price, but there’s nothing opaque about the holiday-season pattern—they likely priced in some small risk of the song underperforming or chart changes.

4. MISPRICING ASSESSMENT  
Given the near-certainty of another holiday #1 run in Dec 2026, the true probability is closer to ~98%. Pricing at 85% implies a ~13 cent undervaluation. There’s no hidden technicality–the resolution window includes the holiday season–and Billboard data shows the seasonal pattern is highly reliable.

5. EXPECTED VALUE CALCULATION  
Strength of evidence: 70% (strong Billboard-based pattern but some minimal execution risk)  
Magnitude of mispricing: ~13 cents  
Expected value = 0.70 × 13 c = 9.1 c ≈ 9.1 c

EXPECTED_VALUE: 9.1  
SUMMARY: Mariah’s “All I Want for Christmas Is You” has reliably reclaimed #1 each holiday since 2019, and the final chart window (data Dec 25–31, 2026) almost certainly will feature it at #1. The market’s 85% undervalues this near-certainty by roughly 13 cents.  
CONFIDENCE: medium
analysis.summary: Mariah’s “All I Want for Christmas Is You” has reliably reclaimed #1 each holiday since 2019, and the final chart window (data Dec 25–31, 2026) almost certainly will feature it at #1. The market’s 85% undervalues this near-certainty by roughly 13 cents.

0x238e76ab8673302f69465f6276dc30397f2da6.question: Will Derek Merrin be the Republican nominee for OH-09?

analysis.question: Will Derek Merrin be the Republican nominee for OH-09?
analysis.fullAnalysis:
1. CONTEXT ANALYSIS  
The market asks whether Derek Merrin will be the Republican nominee in the OH-09 primary on May 5, 2026. A “YES” outcome requires that Merrin officially secures the GOP nomination; a “NO” covers any other candidate or failure to announce by November 3, 2026.

2. EVIDENCE EVALUATION  
Bullish on Merrin (>36.5%):  
- None of the research specifically highlights Merrin as the frontrunner.  
- Four “dynamic” candidates are in the race, suggesting a competitive primary; if Merrin has stronger name recognition or fundraising than peers, 36.5% may understate his chances—but no direct data on that.  

Bearish on Merrin (<36.5%):  
- NRCC coverage discusses multiple GOP hopefuls without naming Merrin, implying he’s not the clear favorite.  
- No party endorsement has been reported for OH-09, and strong GOP endorsements elsewhere hint at an open field.  
- Typical four-way primaries default to near-equal odds (~25% each), so 36.5% could be an overestimate.  

Source credibility and recency: All materials are from February 2026 and local/regional in focus. None directly assess Merrin, so our inference relies on absence of evidence rather than positive signals.

3. MARKET EFFICIENCY ANALYSIS  
- A 36.5% price might reflect one or two traders who know of Merrin’s organizational strength, while the broader market is quiet.  
- Low $2.3k volume and narrow spread suggest price-setting by few participants; lack of public polling or endorsements likely keeps others on the sidelines.  

4. MISPRICING ASSESSMENT  
- There is no concrete positive evidence boosting Merrin above 30–35%.  
- By defaulting to equal-chance logic and lack of endorsement, 36.5% may be 10 cents too high.  
- However, absence of detailed fundraising or internal polling means this is speculative.  

5. EXPECTED VALUE CALCULATION  
- Strength of evidence: ~20% (weak, inference from silence rather than direct data)  
- Magnitude of mispricing: ~10 cents (36.5% vs. a baseline ~25–30%)  
- Expected value = 0.20 × 10 cents = 2 cents  

EXPECTED_VALUE: 2  
SUMMARY: There’s no direct data on Merrin’s standing; in a four-way GOP primary one might assign him ~25% odds, making 36.5% appear ~10 cents high. However, the evidence is weak and largely based on absence of mention, so any mispricing is speculative and small.  
CONFIDENCE: low

0xa91ddfc41b279099da4d4ab260fdc50ed43c09.question: Anthony Albanese out as Prime Minister of Australia by December 31, 2026?

analysis.question: Anthony Albanese out as Prime Minister of Australia by December 31, 2026?
analysis.fullAnalysis:
1. CONTEXT ANALYSIS  
The market asks whether Anthony Albanese will cease being Australia’s Prime Minister by December 31, 2026. “Yes” resolves on any announced resignation/removal before that date; otherwise “No.” Removing Albanese mid-term would require either a party room coup, major scandal, health issues, or unforeseen catastrophe.

2. EVIDENCE EVALUATION  
Bullish for “No” (supports ~80% chance he stays):  
- Roy Morgan (Feb 2026): ALP at 58.5% 2PP vs Coalition 41.5%, indicating strong government popularity.  
- Newspoll (Bonham, Mar 1 2026): Labor leads 58–42 2PP; Albanese retains PM-Better rating (albeit modestly).  
- Coalition disarray, weak opposition and early election not until mid-2027, making mid-term ouster unlikely absent dramatic event.  
Bearish for “Yes” (risks >0):  
- Albanese net satisfaction is low; leadership spills are plausible in Westminster systems.  
- Carney (Feb 25 2026): cost-of-living pressures and budget expectations could erode support if handled poorly.  
- One Nation’s rise could fragment support and embolden internal challengers.  
All sources are recent and credible; none point to immediate removal but highlight some vulnerability. All appear already factored into the ~20% pricetag.

3. MARKET EFFICIENCY ANALYSIS  
A 20.5% probability plausibly reflects consensus that removal is unlikely but non-negligible given two-year horizon. Low volume leaves open the possibility of a handful of traders setting this, but the price mirrors intuitive baseline risk (~10–25%) for a PM in mid-term. The narrow spread suggests at least some engagement, not pure apathy.

4. MISPRICING ASSESSMENT  
There is no clear, overlooked catalyst or piece of intelligence indicating the true probability is markedly above or below 20.5%. Political volatility exists, but major destabilizers would likely be public and priced in once evident. Absent evidence of an imminent leadership challenge or scandal, 20.5% is reasonable.

5. EXPECTED VALUE CALCULATION  
Strength of evidence that a large mispricing exists: ~20% (I find little missing).  
Magnitude of mispricing if any: ~5 cents.  
Expected value ≈ 0.2 × 5 c = 1 cent.

EXPECTED_VALUE: 1  
SUMMARY: Recent polls and analysis show the ALP in strong position and no clear, unpriced catalyst for Albanese’s removal by end-2026. A ~20% probability aligns with modest leadership-challenge risk over two years, suggesting no evident mispricing.  
CONFIDENCE: medium
analysis.summary: Recent polls and analysis show the ALP in strong position and no clear, unpriced catalyst for Albanese’s removal by end-2026. A ~20% probability aligns with modest leadership-challenge risk over two years, suggesting no evident mispricing.

analysis.question: Will Republicans lose House majority before the midterms?
analysis.fullAnalysis:
1. CONTEXT ANALYSIS  
This market asks whether the GOP will at any point lose its House majority before polls open on Nov 3, 2026. As of 3/1/26 Republicans hold a 218–214 majority with 3 vacancies, so they’d need to cede at least two seats to Democrats in special elections (or win fewer new seats than Democrats) before Nov 3 2026 for “Yes.” Mere retirements or vacancies alone won’t flip control because the majority threshold adjusts for vacant seats.

2. EVIDENCE EVALUATION  
– Evidence suggesting mispricing:  
  • Nate Silver’s generic ballot (D +5.5) implies Democrats are favored broadly, but that informs the general election more than sporadic special elections.  
  • NPR notes GOP losses in recent special elections—but doesn’t quantify enough to expect two flips in the next ~20 months.  
  • High retirements (Chuck Todd) signal national mood but don’t create immediate vacancies.  

– Evidence supporting 15.5%:  
  • Republicans’ razor-thin one-seat margin leaves little room for error. Special elections are relatively few; losing two seats before Nov 2026 is a low-probability event.  
  • Variability in polls (Silver) and mixed public sentiment (Leiter et al.) add uncertainty but not enough to make flips likely soon.  

– Source credibility/recency: All sources are from Feb 2026 (recent) and reputable (Silver, NPR, Cook-type analyses). None provide hard forecasts of special-election losses.  

3. MARKET EFFICIENCY ANALYSIS  
15.5% can be justified as the consensus probability that Republicans will lose two special elections in the coming 20 months. Low volume likely reflects the specialized nature of the question (special elections, not the general midterms) and that few traders focus on intra-term House flips.  

4. MISPRICING ASSESSMENT  
No clear, missing data: we have polls pointing to a D advantage, but special-election dynamics differ from general elections. There’s no obvious news of upcoming wave special elections or large numbers of resignations. The need to lose two seats makes a flip unlikely. The 15.5% price appears reasonable, with no strong evidence it’s off by more than a few cents.  

5. EXPECTED VALUE CALCULATION  
Strength of evidence that price is off: ~30% (weak).  
Magnitude of mispricing if wrong: ~5¢.  
Expected value = 0.30 × 5¢ = 1.5¢  

EXPECTED_VALUE: 1.5  
SUMMARY: Republicans need to lose two seats via special elections before Nov 3, 2026 to lose their majority. Research shows Democratic polling strength but no concrete path to two special-election flips, so a 15.5% price seems reasonable.  
CONFIDENCE: medium
analysis.summary: Republicans need to lose two seats via special elections before Nov 3, 2026 to lose their majority. Research shows Democratic polling strength but no concrete path to two special-election flips, so a 15.5% price seems reasonable.

0x096f26b2abcfde3ee7863e7916ca1768252271.question: Will Democrats win all "core four" senate races?

analysis.question: Will Democrats win all "core four" senate races?
analysis.fullAnalysis:
1. CONTEXT ANALYSIS  
This market pays out if the Democratic nominee wins all four 2026 U.S. Senate races in Georgia, Michigan, North Carolina, and Maine (including any run-offs). A “YES” requires a Dem flip in Georgia, Michigan, and North Carolina plus a Dem hold in Maine; any other result is “NO.”

2. EVIDENCE EVALUATION  
Bearish evidence  
- A covers.com prediction-market composite (Feb. 20) gives Democrats only a 41% chance to win overall Senate control, implying a lower chance just to flip four seats once you factor in holding current seats.  
- A CNN analysis (Feb. 4) stresses the map favors Republicans, notes North Carolina “has not elected a Democrat in years,” and calls GOP advantage “challenging.”  
- Washington Monthly (Feb. 13) highlights internal divisions in candidate selection for battlegrounds and generational splits in Maine, suggesting extra uncertainty in these races.  

Bullish evidence  
- CNN also notes strong Democratic recruitment, a motivated base driven by cost-of-living issues, and negative voter sentiment on Trump’s economic policies.  
- In Maine, the incumbent Dem governor (Janet Mills) enjoys sizeable support, making that seat relatively safe.  
- Broader commentary (Kristol/Brownstein, Feb. 8) points to demographic shifts and potential Democratic momentum.  

Source credibility/recency: All cited articles are from reputable outlets in Feb. 2026. None supply concrete state-level probabilities for the core four.  

3. MARKET EFFICIENCY ANALYSIS  
A 62.5% price could reflect an informed consensus that flipping these four particular seats is easier than winning the broader Senate map (control markets sit at ~41%). Low volume suggests price may be set by few traders—not necessarily a broad arbitrage. But it could also indicate no major mispricing if no new state-level polls have dramatically shifted probabilities.

4. MISPRICING ASSESSMENT  
There is no direct polling or odds for these exact four races in the research, and the closest proxy (Senate control markets) is for a larger event. The difference between a 41% control probability and 62.5% for these four is explainable (our event is narrower). Thus, the evidence does not decisively show a >20¢ mispricing.  

5. EXPECTED VALUE CALCULATION  
Strength of evidence that the price is wrong: ~30% (we lack granular state polls).  
Magnitude of potential mispricing: ~10¢ (loosely implied by control vs. core-four divergence).  
Expected value = 0.30 × 10¢ = 3¢

EXPECTED_VALUE: 3  
SUMMARY: Available research offers only broad control-market and qualitative analysis—no state-specific odds—so a 62.5% price for winning the four core races is not clearly out of line. The 41% Senate-control estimate isn’t directly comparable, and there is no solid polling data missing from the market price.  
CONFIDENCE: low

The 41% Senate-control estimate isn’t directly comparable, and there is no solid polling data missing from the market price.

0x2454c412d48443a73e15d93ae01e20caefd5d6.question: Will Kevin Warsh be confirmed as Fed Chair and will rates hit 2.5% or lower in 2026?

analysis.question: Will Kevin Warsh be confirmed as Fed Chair and will rates hit 2.5% or lower in 2026?
analysis.fullAnalysis:
1. CONTEXT ANALYSIS  
The market pays out YES only if (a) Kevin Warsh is confirmed by the Senate as Fed Chair by 12/31/26 AND (b) the lower bound of the Fed’s target federal funds rate ever reaches 2.5% or below at any time in 2026. Otherwise it resolves NO.  

2. EVIDENCE EVALUATION  
Bullish Warsh confirmation:  
• PredictStreet (Feb 9) shows a 94.8% market probability he’ll be nominated; Senate confirmation risk is the only remaining hurdle.  
• Confirmation before June is widely expected; no significant Senate opposition is reported.  
Hawkish rate outlook:  
• Jefferson City News Tribune (Feb 27) and Kitco News (Feb 17) report a bullish economy (IMF growth 2.4%, 4% unemployment) and a Fed inclined to only 0.5% total cuts in 2026, keeping rates ≫2.5%.  
• These views imply <10% chance of rates falling to ≤2.5% in 2026.  
Bearish (deep‐cut) scenarios:  
• Fed Governor Stephen Miran speculates cuts to 2.0–2.25% by year‐end 2026. Such a minority view implies up to ~25% chance of hitting ≤2.5%.  
Source credibility/recency: all sources are February 2026, credible for short‐term Fed prospects.  

3. MARKET EFFICIENCY ANALYSIS  
A ~95% chance of confirmation × a ~25% chance of deep cuts yields ~23.8%, almost exactly the 23.5% price. Low volume may simply reflect that informed traders have already modeled both mainstream and minority views.  

4. MISPRICING ASSESSMENT  
There is no glaring missing factor. Mainstream Fed forecasts suggest rates won’t drop that low, but a plausible minority scenario of aggressive cuts under Warsh accounts for the ~25% rate‐cut component. The 23.5% price cleanly embeds 95%×25%.  

5. EXPECTED VALUE CALCULATION  
Strength of evidence that price is fair (so mispricing is small): 40%  
Potential mispricing magnitude: ~5¢  
Expected value ≈ 0.40 × 5¢ = 2¢  

EXPECTED_VALUE: 2  
SUMMARY: The current 23.5% price aligns with a ~95% chance of Warsh confirmation multiplied by a plausible ~25% chance of deep rate cuts to ≤2.5% by year-end 2026. Mainstream forecasts suggest much smaller cuts, but the market appears to price the minority deep‐cut scenario, indicating no clear mispricing.  
CONFIDENCE: medium
analysis.summary: The current 23.5% price aligns with a ~95% chance of Warsh confirmation multiplied by a plausible ~25% chance of deep rate cuts to ≤2.5% by year-end 2026. Mainstream forecasts suggest much smaller cuts, but the market appears to price the minority deep‐cut scenario, indicating no clear mispricing.

0x9d1f2f0054e31e702ab533a0f6ab859c764052.question: Will Trump act to ban mail-in voting or voting machines by June 30?

analysis.question: Will Trump act to ban mail-in voting or voting machines by June 30?
analysis.fullAnalysis:
1. CONTEXT ANALYSIS  
The market asks whether Trump will, by June 30, 2026, sign federal legislation or take an executive action that effectively bans or inhibits mail-in voting or voting machines for the 2026 midterms. A “Yes” requires a formal act—an executive order, memorandum, or signed bill—targeting mail-in ballots or the use of voting machines. Anything short of a binding federal measure (e.g., mere rhetoric or nonbinding guidance) resolves as “No.”

2. EVIDENCE EVALUATION  
Bullish evidence (supports >65.5%):  
• Feb 27 New Republic: A draft executive order is circulating among MAGA circles, showing concrete planning to declare a national emergency and restrict mail-in voting and machines.  
• Feb 19 New Republic: Trump’s rally rhetoric explicitly calls for eliminating mail-in ballots except for military/disabled voters, indicating high intent.  
• CSMonitor Feb 27: Several GOP-led states already limit mail-in voting, suggesting a partisan appetite for federal action and a supportive base.  

Bearish evidence (supports <65.5%):  
• New Republic Feb 27: Trump publicly denies planning to issue such an order soon, raising questions about timing or follow-through.  
• KOTA Feb 25: Any federal executive order would face legal challenges and Congress lacks clear authority over state election rules; bifurcation by states could blunt impact.  
• PolitiFact Nov 3, 2025: While Trump criticizes mail-in voting, no concrete federal proposals have been introduced in Congress, and GOP unity on bold federal action is uncertain.  

Source credibility/recency: All are major outlets with reporting in the past month, although some rely on drafts or rhetoric rather than finalized actions.  

3. MARKET EFFICIENCY ANALYSIS  
A 65.5% price plausibly reflects genuine consensus that Trump intends to push aggressively on election rules—enough that some executive order or directive is likely before the deadline—but also accounts for significant legal and institutional hurdles. Low volume could indicate either that the outcome seems moderately likely (drawing limited speculative interest) or that few specialists have engaged. The tight spread suggests existing traders feel information is already incorporated.

4. MISPRICING ASSESSMENT  
There is no standout evidence that a final executive action is either virtually certain (which would push price toward ~90%) or highly unlikely (<40%). The draft order and Trump’s rhetoric support a better-than-even chance, but persistent denials, congressional obstacles, and judicial risks cap the probability. Overall, 65.5% falls in the plausible middle; I see no >20-point mispricing given the balance of bullish vs. bearish factors.

5. EXPECTED VALUE CALCULATION  
Strength of evidence that price is significantly off: ~20% (balanced and inconclusive)  
Magnitude of possible mispricing: ~5 cents  
Expected value = 20% × 5 cents = 1 cent

EXPECTED_VALUE: 1  
SUMMARY: The balance of recent draft orders and strong rhetoric suggests Trump is likely to attempt a ban, but public denials, legal hurdles, and lack of enacted measures keep the odds far from certain. A 65.5% probability reasonably reflects these offsetting factors.  
CONFIDENCE: medium
analysis.summary: The balance of recent draft orders and strong rhetoric suggests Trump is likely to attempt a ban, but public denials, legal hurdles, and lack of enacted measures keep the odds far from certain. A 65.5% probability reasonably reflects these offsetting factors.

Plausible magnitude of mispricing: 10 cents

An 18% YES probability implies the market assigns nearly one-in-five odds to a surprise drop into 3.00%–3.49%. Given consensus ~4%, that seems slightly generous to the YES side—but not wildly so. Low volume suggests limited liquidity rather than a strong contrarian view.

4. MISPRICING ASSESSMENT  
No single report suggests a >20-point mispricing. The market’s ~18% plausibly captures tail risk of a deeper slowdown or unexpected disinflation. With no clear data missing, we can’t conclude a significant mispricing.

Strength of evidence (that market isn’t badly mispriced): ~60%  
Magnitude of potential mispricing: ~5 cents  
Expected value ≈ 0.60 × 5 ¢ = 3 ¢

analysis.question: Will SpaceX IPO by December 31, 2026?
analysis.fullAnalysis:
1. CONTEXT ANALYSIS  
The market asks whether SpaceX completes an IPO by December 31, 2026. For YES, SpaceX must file, gain SEC approval, and list equity on a recognized exchange by that date. A public acquisition of SpaceX instead would resolve as NO.

2. EVIDENCE EVALUATION  
Bullish evidence:  
• AInvest (2026-02-28) reports SpaceX plans to file in March 2026, targeting a mid-2026 IPO and $1.5 trillion valuation.  
• ValueTheMarkets (2026-01-30) notes stable Starlink revenues and private valuations on par with public aerospace firms; Kalshi markets already price a 63% chance of an announcement by October.  
• Multiple outlets (Nasdaq, Investing.com, AInvest) concur on a plausible 2026 IPO, driven by Starlink momentum and investor demand.  

Bearish evidence:  
• Elon Musk’s public comments that SpaceX “isn’t in a rush” could signal strategic delays.  
• Execution risks—Starship setbacks, regulatory approvals, market volatility—could push the IPO past year-end.  
• AInvest’s mid-February article highlights that flawless execution and favorable market sentiment are assumed; any deviation could derail the timeline.  

Source credibility is high: major industry outlets and specialized investment analysts with recent publication dates. No single source provides a decisive counterargument to the 2026 timeline; bearish points largely note generic risks likely already factored in.

3. MARKET EFFICIENCY ANALYSIS  
An 85% price implies traders believe remaining hurdles (regulatory, execution) have only a ~15% chance of causing a delay. Given multiple independent reports of a firm filing timeline and revenue backdrop, this seems a reasonable consensus rather than a lone over-confident trader. Low volume likely reflects specialized interest, not controversy over the outcome.

4. MISPRICING ASSESSMENT  
There is no obvious missing information that would shift the probability by >10–15%. Execution and market‐condition risks are well documented and appear priced in via the ~15% tail risk. The 85% probability aligns with the after-October bump from Kalshi’s 63% pre-October estimate plus added confidence for a full calendar year. No credible evidence suggests a sizeable mispricing.

5. EXPECTED VALUE CALCULATION  
Strength of evidence that price is efficient: 70% (reports are cohesive and recent)  
Magnitude of mispricing if any: ~5 cents  
Expected value = 0.70 × 5 cents = 3.5 cents

EXPECTED_VALUE: 3.5  
SUMMARY: The consensus of recent filings, revenue stability, and market sentiment supports an ~85% chance of a 2026 IPO, with documented execution and regulatory risks already factored in. No clear evidence of a significant mispricing emerges.  
CONFIDENCE: medium

analysis.question: Will North and South Korea engage in direct talks by June 30?
analysis.fullAnalysis:
1. CONTEXT ANALYSIS  
The market asks whether North and South Korea will hold direct, government-to-government talks (in-person, phone, or virtual) by June 30, 2026, without a third party relaying messages. A “Yes” requires a publicly acknowledged meeting or call between representatives from both Koreas; routine deconfliction or backchannels do not count.

2. EVIDENCE EVALUATION  
Bearish indicators (supporting low probability ≈15.5%):  
• Yonhap (2026-03-01): President Lee’s public plea for dialogue underscores that no talks have occurred; it shows Seoul pushing, but offers no commitment from Pyongyang.  
• CNA (2026-03-01): Reiterates South Korea’s call but stresses uncertainty and dependent on NK’s response and wider geopolitics.  
• Korea Times (2026-02-26): Focus on U.S.–NK summit prospects, not inter-Korean talks; openness between Washington and Pyongyang may not translate directly into Seoul–Pyongyang channels.  
• Historical NK–US context (Wikipedia): No formal ties, Swedish intermediary in place; suggests inter-Korean direct talks face similar structural hurdles.  

Bullish indicators (could push probability higher):  
• NK’s conditional openness to U.S. dialogue might create diplomatic momentum for Seoul–Pyongyang contact.  
• South Korean government’s strong public push could yield an incentive or face-saving offer from North Korea.  

Source credibility and recency are strong for March 1 materials (Yonhap, CNA). The oldest piece (Hani 2024-11-28) discusses Trump and is only indirectly relevant. None of these sources point to imminent inter-Korean talks, and each highlight prevailing uncertainty.

3. MARKET EFFICIENCY ANALYSIS  
A 15.5% price can reflect consensus that talks are unlikely but not impossible. Low volume suggests few traders have engaged, but the tight spread indicates those who did share a similar view. The outcome is not trivial—if talks were widely expected, more volume would likely pile in; the low volume may instead reflect genuine consensus that the risk is small. Alternatively, it could reflect limited attention by Korea specialists.

4. MISPRICING ASSESSMENT  
No clear sign of a >20-cent mispricing. Evidence leans bearish or neutral, matching a sub-20% probability. There is no overlooked recent diplomatic breakthrough in the research. The uncertainty window (four months) and geopolitical dynamics justify a low but nonzero price. The most charitable interpretation is that informed traders already factored in South Korea’s outreach and NK’s cautious stance.

5. EXPECTED VALUE CALCULATION  
Strength of evidence that price is off: 20% (weak).  
Magnitude of mispricing if present: 5¢ (small).  
Expected value = 20% × 5¢ = 1.0¢

EXPECTED_VALUE: 1.0  
SUMMARY: Current research shows Seoul’s calls for dialogue but no reciprocal commitment from Pyongyang, aligning with a low chance of direct talks by June 30. The market price of 15.5% appears to reflect this cautious consensus, with no strong evidence of mispricing.  
CONFIDENCE: medium

• While headwinds exist, the balance of publicly available evidence points toward at least one 2026 meeting.  
• Given the question’s simplicity and credible evidence on both sides, 69% seems a reasonable consensus estimate.

0xd2e96e78b5a59e12b4d60d6b8426aa93dc6edf.question: Will Trump's approval rating hit 35% in 2026?

analysis.question: Will Trump's approval rating hit 35% in 2026?
analysis.fullAnalysis:
1. CONTEXT ANALYSIS  
The market asks whether, at any point in 2026, Silver Bulletin’s finalized approval rating for Donald Trump will be ≤ 35%. A “Yes” requires at least one data point in 2026 at or below 35%. A “No” means every finalized Silver Bulletin rating throughout 2026 stays above 35%.

2. EVIDENCE EVALUATION  
Bullish (supports a drop to ≤ 35%):  
- Multiple polls show a steady decline: Pew (Jan 29) at 37%, Strength In Numbers/Verasight (Feb 24) at 37%, CNN (Feb 23) at 36%, and Qantas mid-Feb at 43% but with a strong downward trend since 2025.  
- Silver Bulletin reports net approval weakening to –14.9; strong disapproval up to 45%. Poll‐to‐poll volatility (~1–2%) makes a dip below 35% plausible at least once.  
Bearish (supports no drop below 35%):  
- Current polling averages in the high-30s, not yet ≤ 35%. No major new scandal or crisis is widely anticipated that would drive a further steep fall. Historical U.S. presidential approvals tend to stabilize around an equilibrium with limited short-term swings.  
Source credibility: Pew, Reuters/Ipsos, CNN, and Nate Silver’s aggregation are all high credibility and very recent (Jan–Feb 2026).  

3. MARKET EFFICIENCY ANALYSIS  
A 30% probability fits a scenario where traders recognize the downward trend but also note his approval heavy base in the high-30s and limited evidence of an imminent shock. Low volume may simply reflect that this is viewed as a niche question with a straightforward, moderately uncertain outcome rather than a grossly mispriced opportunity.

4. MISPRICING ASSESSMENT  
No clear, unpriced catalyst emerges from the research. The ~2-point gap from current ~37% polls to the 35% threshold, combined with historic polling volatility, reasonably yields a roughly one-in-three chance of dipping to ≤ 35% at least once. There is no specific credible evidence that this probability should be materially higher or lower by more than a few cents.

5. EXPECTED VALUE CALCULATION  
Strength of evidence that mispricing exists: 30%  
Magnitude of mispricing if any: 5¢  
Expected value = 0.30 × 5¢ = 1.5¢

EXPECTED_VALUE: 1.5  
SUMMARY: Recent high-credibility polls show Trump’s approval drifting in the high-30s with a modest downward trend but no clear catalyst for a fall to ≤ 35% with more than ~30% probability. The current 30% market price appears reasonable given poll volatility and historical stability.  
CONFIDENCE: low

0x17ba5a7a08bd3f7f87487285c5713e305b8fc9.question: Will a US ally get a nuke before 2027?

analysis.question: Will a US ally get a nuke before 2027?
analysis.fullAnalysis:
1. CONTEXT ANALYSIS  
The market asks whether any NATO member or Major Non-NATO Ally (MNNNA) that was non-nuclear as of November 12, 2025, will officially possess an independently controlled operational nuclear weapon by December 31, 2026. “Yes” resolution requires credible confirmation; mere participation in nuclear sharing (e.g., NATO nuclear deployments) or enrichment programs without weaponization does not count. Israel is explicitly excluded.  

2. EVIDENCE EVALUATION  
Bullish signals (supporting a higher probability than 15.5%):  
• 2026-02-20 AP report on Saudi Arabia exploring uranium enrichment under a U.S. deal. Saudi Arabia is a MNNNA, and while the current deal is for peaceful uses, expanding enrichment capacity lowers technical barriers if Riyadh decided to weaponize.  
• Just Security (2026-02-05): Geopolitical fragmentation and U.S. retreat from multilateralism could encourage U.S. allies to pursue nuclear weapons as self-help deterrents.  

Bearish signals (supporting equal or lower probability):  
• ChinaTalk (2025-04-30): A market prediction for 2025 proliferation stands at 6%; extending the horizon to end-2026 raises the odds but not dramatically. The article emphasizes strong U.S. extended deterrence, which disincentivizes allies from Go-It-Alone programs.  
• Bulletin of the Atomic Scientists (2025-01-13): U.S. nuclear modernization and stronger security commitments make allies less likely to incur the diplomatic and economic costs of independent arsenals.  
• Carnegie Endowment (2025-04-17): In the absence of an acute security shock, nuclear acquisitions remain a high-cost, politically fraught endeavor; forecasting escalation risks doesn’t translate directly into actual proliferation by allies.  

Source credibility and recency: AP News (Feb 2026) and Just Security (Feb 2026) are recent and credible for forward-looking risk. Schneider & Ottinger (Apr 2025) and Carnegie (Apr 2025) offer strong strategic context but are older. All sources appear reliable on their respective topics.

No single piece of research indicates a probability dramatically above or below the current 15.5%. The Saudi enrichment story is notable but does not yet signal an irreversible path to weapons, and U.S. extended deterrence remains a powerful counterforce.

3. MARKET EFFICIENCY ANALYSIS  
• A 15.5% price could reflect a consensus that proliferation risk rises modestly in 2026 compared to the 6% 2025 forecast.  
• Low volume ($1.9K over 108 days) suggests few active traders; the tight 3¢ spread indicates those traders agree on a mid-teens probability.  
• Visibility issues and structural barriers (e.g., complex technical and political hurdles for allies to get bombs) likely deter large-scale trading, rather than a glaring mispricing that informed players would pounce on.

4. MISPRICING ASSESSMENT  
• No research finding points to a >20-cent mispricing. The bullish scenario (Saudi pivot) and bearish scenario (U.S. deterrence) roughly offset.  
• The long time horizon (10 months left) entails genuine uncertainty; political or security shocks could shift odds quickly.  
• The current price plausibly anticipates modest growth in proliferation risk without overreacting to preliminary enrichment talks.

5. EXPECTED VALUE CALCULATION  
Strength of evidence for mispricing: 30% (there’s balanced but limited directional signal).  
Magnitude of potential mispricing: 5 cents (unlikely to be >10¢).  
Expected value = 0.30 × 5¢ = 1.5¢

EXPECTED_VALUE: 1.5  
SUMMARY: Research shows balanced bullish (e.g., Saudi enrichment talks) and bearish (U.S. deterrence strength) signals, suggesting the 15.5% price reasonably reflects moderate proliferation risk by end-2026. There’s no clear, large mispricing evident given the low-volume environment and genuine uncertainty.  
CONFIDENCE: medium
analysis.summary: Research shows balanced bullish (e.g., Saudi enrichment talks) and bearish (U.S. deterrence strength) signals, suggesting the 15.5% price reasonably reflects moderate proliferation risk by end-2026. There’s no clear, large mispricing evident given the low-volume environment and genuine uncertainty.

0x81970d6af0f7545b39c5a98b032cca14cdb9b4.question: Will the New York Islanders make the NHL Playoffs?

analysis.question: Will the New York Islanders make the NHL Playoffs?
analysis.fullAnalysis:
1. CONTEXT ANALYSIS  
This market asks whether the New York Islanders will finish the 2025–26 regular season qualified for the NHL playoffs under official rules (top three in division + two wild cards per conference). A “Yes” requires the Islanders to finish high enough in the Eastern Conference standings—either a top-three spot in the Metropolitan Division or one of the two conference wild cards. A “No” if they fail to secure one of those berths or become mathematically eliminated before season’s end.

2. EVIDENCE EVALUATION  
Bullish evidence (supports ≥69.5%):  
• Current 34-21-5 record (73 pts), .567 win pct., on a four-game winning streak, 7-3 in last 10—indicates form.  
• ProHockeyRumors (2/28) labels Isles as buyers at trade deadline with $6M cap space and solid prospects, likely to add depth.  
• BetMGM odds of –165 imply ~62% raw chance (≈60% after removing vig), suggesting public sportsbooks already see >½ chance.  
• Historical consistency: 29 playoff appearances, made in 2024, strong organizational culture under coach Trotz.

Bearish evidence (supports <69.5%):  
• Metropolitan Division is very competitive—Hurricanes, Rangers, Penguins, Devils all vying for spots; small swings in results can shift standings.  
• Isles’ scoring has been inconsistent; injuries (e.g. Bo Horvat) have impacted offense in parts of season.  
• Tight point race—73 pts only good for 3rd or lower in the division; wild-card races elsewhere may outpace them.  
• BetMGM fair probability (~60%) is ~10 points below 69.5%, hinting the prediction market may be over-optimistic unless it prices in trades beyond public view.  

Source credibility & recency: All data from Feb–Mar 2026, from reputable outlets (NHL.com, ProHockeyRumors, BetMGM). No stale or dubious sources.

3. MARKET EFFICIENCY ANALYSIS  
• A 69.5% price could reflect consensus that the Isles will both maintain form and improve via deadline acquisitions—publicly discussed but with uncertain impact.  
• Low $977 volume & 3¢ spread suggest few active participants; price may thus overstate consensus if driven by one or two confident traders.  
• Alternatively, low volume might signal that informed hockey bettors view the outcome as “obvious,” leaving little incentive to trade small edge.

4. MISPRICING ASSESSMENT  
• The main quantitative anchor is BetMGM’s ~60% implied fair probability. The prediction market’s 69.5% is ~9.5¢ higher.  
• The gap may be justified by potential trade-deadline upgrades not fully captured in sportsbook odds, but those are widely reported and likely already priced in.  
• No hard new information (e.g., an unexpected blockbuster trade or a season-ending injury) has emerged since the odds published.  
• Thus, I see only modest overpricing—far below 20¢. A mispricing on the order of 5–8¢ is plausible but not strongly certain.

5. EXPECTED VALUE CALCULATION  
Strength of evidence that price should be closer to ~60%: ~40% (moderately reliable sportsbook anchor versus public reporting on trades).  
Magnitude of mispricing: ~8¢ (difference between 69.5¢ and an adjusted ~61–62¢).  
Expected value = 0.40 × 8¢ = 3.2¢

EXPECTED_VALUE: 3.2  
SUMMARY: The market’s 69.5% likely overstates the Islanders’ true playoff odds (~60% implied by BetMGM after vig). Low volume suggests limited liquidity, and no new information justifies a near-70% price. Any mispricing appears modest (~5–8¢).  
CONFIDENCE: medium
analysis.summary: The market’s 69.5% likely overstates the Islanders’ true playoff odds (~60% implied by BetMGM after vig). Low volume suggests limited liquidity, and no new information justifies a near-70% price. Any mispricing appears modest (~5–8¢).

• SportsbookReview (Feb 24) lists Werenski as co-favorite with Cale Makar at –105 odds, implying ~49% true win probability even after adjusting for juice.  
• Multiple analytics pieces (theScore Feb 18; 1st Ohio Battery Feb 11) highlight Werenski leading the league in ice time, outpacing Makar in points-per-game (1.19 vs. 1.04), and anchoring a playoff-contending Blue Jackets squad.

Bearish/neutral evidence (supporting a lower probability around 40%):

• Team success in the final stretch (Makar’s Avalanche looks surer to clinch, boosting his resume vs. a middling Blue Jackets team) could shift voter sentiment back toward established names.

All sources are recent (Feb 2026) and from credible outlets. None of the bullish points appear “obvious” enough to have been fully priced in—sports bettors still handicap Werenski around ~49%, but this prediction market sits at 42.1%.  

3. MARKET EFFICIENCY ANALYSIS  
A 42.1% price could reflect:  
• A thin market where one or two under-weighting traders set the price, not a broad consensus.

While voters’ final ballots and playoff run outcomes could swing probabilities, none of that material is hidden from both bettors and this market.  

5. EXPECTED VALUE CALCULATION  
Strength of evidence: 50% (moderately strong, given multiple independent sources aligning)  
Magnitude of mispricing: ~7 cents  
Expected value ≈ 0.50 × 7 cents = 3.5 cents  

EXPECTED_VALUE: 3.5  
SUMMARY: Current sportsbook odds (~49% implied) and multiple analytics pieces position Werenski as a co-favorite, suggesting the market’s 42.1% price underestimates his win probability by about 6–7 cents. However, low volume and competition from Makar and Hughes cap the mispricing at a modest level.

analysis.question: Will Renovación Popular (RP) win the most seats in the 2026 Peruvian Chamber of Deputies election?
analysis.fullAnalysis:
1. CONTEXT ANALYSIS  
The market asks whether Renovación Popular (RP) will finish with more seats than any other party in Peru’s April 12, 2026 Chamber of Deputies election. A YES requires RP to outpoll all other parties in seat counts (with tiebreakers by vote totals then alphabetical order). A NO encompasses any other party topping RP or unresolved results by October 31.

2. EVIDENCE EVALUATION  
Bullish signals (in favor of YES):  
• Highly fragmented field (36 parties) means the leading party could win with a relatively low vote/share—potentially in the mid‐teens—so a party polling around 12% today could realistically emerge on top if no other party consolidates.  
• Political instability and crime concerns give outsiders an opening; if RP crafts a compelling security‐focused message, it might surge among undecided voters.

Bearish signals (in favor of NO):  
• Datum poll (Feb 1, 2026) shows RP at just 11.9% voter intention versus 42.5% undecided/null, suggesting RP is not currently leading and faces an uphill battle in mobilizing supporters.  
• No publicly available seat projections or strong coalition announcements that would materially boost RP’s chances.  
• Competitive landscape with 36 candidates and reintroduction of a bicameral system means voters focused on the Senate race may dilute RP’s Deputies vote.  

Source credibility: Datum poll and the Americas Quarterly profile are recent, reputable, and directly pertinent. The bicameral analysis adds context but does not directly estimate RP’s performance.  

3. MARKET EFFICIENCY ANALYSIS  
The 32% price could reflect:  
• A consensus among a few traders who believe fragmentation and voter volatility give RP a one‐in‐three chance.  
• Absence of large informed‐money bets, leaving price driven by early, possibly overconfident, speculators.  
• Low volume likely signals lack of deep engagement rather than consensus; an obvious outcome would attract more volume.

4. MISPRICING ASSESSMENT  
There’s no definitive seat‐projection evidence that RP will lead (Datum’s 11.9% suggests they trail). But volatility and fragmentation mean the market may be underestimating RP’s upside. The lack of direct seat‐share modeling implies the 32% price is a rough consensus guess, not a precise estimate. No clear 20+ cent mispricing emerges; if anything, the market may be slightly optimistic but not egregiously so.

5. EXPECTED VALUE CALCULATION  
Strength of evidence: 30% (polling data is suggestive but early, no seat models available)  
Magnitude of mispricing: 10 cents (market could be 22–32%)  
Expected value ≈ 0.3 × 10 c = 3 c  

EXPECTED_VALUE: 3  
SUMMARY: With RP at 11.9% in the latest Datum poll amid a fractured 36‐party field, a 32% chance to top the Deputies seems slightly optimistic but not decisively mispriced, especially given high voter volatility. Low volume suggests limited informed engagement, and seat projections remain uncertain.  
CONFIDENCE: low

Magnitude of mispricing: 5 cents (if any)

Bullish for Republicans (supporting ~67.5% as reasonable):  
- WA-04 has historically leaned strongly Republican, with past GOP victories by double-digit margins.  
- No major redistricting changes have been reported that would flip it; it remains a safe-lean GOP district by raw voter registration and past election results.  
- No public polling yet suggests a competitive upset; without contradictory data, traders may default to the historical baseline.  

All sources are very recent (Jan–Feb 2026) and from credible outlets (Ballotpedia, Almanac, NPR).

3. MARKET EFFICIENCY ANALYSIS  
A 67.5% price can be justified as a consensus balancing a strong GOP baseline against open-seat uncertainty and a potential independent spoiler. The low volume and tight spread suggest a few traders set the price; but it may also reflect consensus that nothing dramatic has changed enough to move it much. Informed traders might simply have no new polling to trade on.

4. MISPRICING ASSESSMENT  
The key negative developments (incumbent retire­ment, independent entry, midterm headwinds) seem likely already factored into the sub-70% probability. There is no specific recent polling or modeling that cuts the GOP chance by more than, say, 5–10 points from 67.5%. Without more concrete data, a large mispricing (>20 points) is implausible. Any small mispricing (<10¢) is likely noise given the lack of fresh, district-level surveys.

5. EXPECTED VALUE CALCULATION  
Strength of evidence that there is a remaining mispricing: 40% (we have plausible but not definitive factors)  
Magnitude of mispricing: 5 cents (we might be overestimating GOP chance slightly)  
Expected value = 0.40 × 5¢ = 2.0¢  

EXPECTED_VALUE: 2.0  
SUMMARY: The retirement of the GOP incumbent and an independent candidate introduce some headwinds, but WA-04’s strong Republican history likely justifies a sub-70% chance for the GOP. There is no clear new polling or redistricting evidence to support a large mispricing.  
CONFIDENCE: medium

0x604dc4df290145ae3e259548bfdd1f7fbdc916.question: Will "Sinners" win exactly 4 awards at the Oscars?

analysis.question: Will "Sinners" win exactly 4 awards at the Oscars?
analysis.fullAnalysis:
1. CONTEXT ANALYSIS  
The market asks whether “Sinners” will win exactly four Oscars at the 98th Academy Awards. A YES requires the film to take home exactly four awards; any other total (0–3 or 5+) resolves NO.  

2. EVIDENCE EVALUATION  
Bullish / Underpricing signals:  
- The Ankler pundits assign per‐category win probabilities summing to an expected 3.3 wins.  A Poisson‐binomial approximation yields roughly a 25% chance of exactly four wins—above the market’s 18%.  
- Key categories (Original Screenplay 77%, Score 69%, Casting 54%, Cinematography 42%, Production Design 23%) suggest plausible multiple wins and moderate odds of hitting exactly four.  

Bearish / Overpricing signals:  
- Competition from other nominees and low odds in several categories (Best Picture 8%, Editing 8%, Makeup 8%, etc.) widen variance; correlation among wins could depress the chance of exactly four.  
- IMDb analyses stress uncertainty around the precise win tally despite strong nomination counts; no expert source pins the 4‐win probability much higher than ~20–25%.  

Source credibility and recency: IMDb and The Ankler (Feb 2026) are reputable and up‐to‐date. No single authoritative forecast quotes a four‐win probability far from market price.  

3. MARKET EFFICIENCY ANALYSIS  
- A mid‐teens to mid‐20s percent probability for exactly four wins is consistent with independent expert estimates.  
- Low $9k volume and narrow bid–ask suggests a few confident traders rather than broad consensus, but also suggests no glaring arbitrage.  
- The outcome isn’t trivial—experts differ and outcome hinges on Oscar night voting—so limited trading may reflect low expected returns rather than mispricing.  

4. MISPRICING ASSESSMENT  
- The gap between an approximate 25% expert‐based estimate and the market’s 18% is under 10¢.  
- Given uncertainties (win correlations, final voter behavior) and lack of absent critical information, there’s no strong evidence of a >20¢ mispricing.  

5. EXPECTED VALUE CALCULATION  
- Strength of evidence (category‐level probabilities): ~50%  
- Magnitude of mispricing (25% − 18% = 7¢)  
- EV = 0.5 × 7¢ = 3.5¢  

EXPECTED_VALUE: 3.5  
SUMMARY: Expert category‐level win odds imply a ~25% chance of exactly four Oscars, close to the market’s 18% and suggesting no large mispricing. Low trading volume likely reflects limited upside, not a missing information arbitrage.  
CONFIDENCE: medium
analysis.summary: Expert category‐level win odds imply a ~25% chance of exactly four Oscars, close to the market’s 18% and suggesting no large mispricing. Low trading volume likely reflects limited upside, not a missing information arbitrage.

0x284e9f6e1308f42c1f4d400b7b3f9614d27a3f.question: Will Gold (GC) hit (HIGH) $7,000 by end of December?

analysis.question: Will Gold (GC) hit (HIGH) $7,000 by end of December?
analysis.fullAnalysis:
1. CONTEXT ANALYSIS  
The market asks whether CME front‐month GC settlement will ever trade at or above \$7,000 on any official settlement day by the last trading day of December 2026. “Yes” requires one daily settlement ≥\$7,000 between now and end-Dec 2026; otherwise it resolves “No.”

2. EVIDENCE EVALUATION  
Bullish evidence for a >46.5% chance:  
• TradingView (Feb 12 2026) cites an “extreme” Fibonacci scenario toward \$7,200 if the current upward trend persists and short rates stay low. This is a single, speculative scenario rather than consensus.  
• Ongoing central-bank buying and safe-haven flows (AInvest Feb 28) could fuel an extended bull run if geopolitical or fiscal stresses intensify.  

Bearish/evidence against >46.5%:  
• Wall-Street consensus (AhaSignals Feb 4) averages \$5,600, with top targets at \$6,300 (JPM), UBS \$6,200, Deutsche \$6,000, and even the wide range ( \$3,450–\$7,150) implies hitting \$7,000 is a tail outcome. The Consensus Density Index of 0.87 signals vulnerability to downside shocks.  
• JPM (Kitco Feb 25) explicitly forecasts \$6,300 by year-end 2026, not 7,000.  
• Kitco (Dec 2025) warns against expecting another 65% return in 2026, making a 38%+ leap from the current \$5,070 to \$7,000 unlikely under base-case conditions.  

Source credibility: All are recent (Feb 2026) reputable financial analysts or major banks. No obvious biases.  

3. MARKET EFFICIENCY ANALYSIS  
A 46.5% price implies near-even odds. That could reflect:  
• A one-sided market where an overconfident bull has placed most volume, skewing the estimate upward.  
• Low liquidity and missing engagement from hedge funds or macro traders, suggesting the price is not the result of broad, informed consensus.  

Alternatively, it could reflect traders assigning a nontrivial tail risk from unforeseen macro shocks (e.g., debt crises, unanchored inflation) that could push gold to record highs. However, most professional forecasts do not incorporate a >40% chance of breaching \$7,000.  

4. MISPRICING ASSESSMENT  
Weighing both sides:  
• Bullish tail‐risk scenarios do exist but appear to be treated as low‐probability by the broader analyst community.  
• Consensus targets cluster ~\$5,600–\$6,300, with very few forecasting ≥\$7,000.  
• No published estimates or modeling support a near-50% likelihood of hitting \$7,000.  

Conclusion: The current 46.5% seems overstated by ~20 percentage points relative to median professional forecasts. There’s credible evidence (bank forecasts, consensus analysis) pointing to a lower probability (<30%).  

5. EXPECTED VALUE CALCULATION  
Strength of evidence that market is overpriced: 60%  
Estimated magnitude of mispricing: 21.5 cents (46.5% – ~25%)  
Expected value = 0.60 × 21.5¢ ≈ 12.9¢ → round to 12.9¢  

EXPECTED_VALUE: 12.9  
SUMMARY: Analyst consensus targets (~\$5,600–\$6,300) and fragility risks imply a <30% odds of gold hitting \$7,000 by Dec 2026, suggesting the 46.5% market price is likely ~20 points too high. Expected value of this insight is ~13¢.  
CONFIDENCE: medium
analysis.summary: Analyst consensus targets (~\$5,600–\$6,300) and fragility risks imply a <30% odds of gold hitting \$7,000 by Dec 2026, suggesting the 46.5% market price is likely ~20 points too high. Expected value of this insight is ~13¢.

0x74778c328104cd816f606f44ca9717fe83036d.question: Will Zelenskyy talk to Putin by December 31?

analysis.question: Will Zelenskyy talk to Putin by December 31?
analysis.fullAnalysis:
1. CONTEXT ANALYSIS  
The market asks whether Volodymyr Zelenskyy and Vladimir Putin will have any direct spoken interaction (in person, by phone, or by video) after the market’s Mar 1, 2026 launch and before Dec 31, 2026 11:59 PM ET. A “Yes” requires at least one qualifying conversation; absent that, it resolves “No.”

2. EVIDENCE EVALUATION  
Bullish signals (suggesting higher than 40.5%):  
• Zelenskyy’s clear public willingness to meet Putin (Sky News, 27 Feb 2026), including calls for stricter sanctions to pressure Russia.  
• Scheduling of U.S.–Russia–Ukraine talks in early March, with plans for a trilateral meeting in Abu Dhabi that could facilitate a bilateral exchange (RFE/RL, 26 Feb).  
• Recognition that video/phone counts, lowering logistical barriers.  

Bearish signals (suggesting lower than 40.5%):  
• No evidence Putin has signaled readiness to talk; repeated warnings that negotiations hinge entirely on his consent (RBC-Ukraine, Interfax).  
• Putin’s military setbacks may harden his stance, and he may view direct talks as weakness (Sky News, 27 Feb).  
• Zelenskyy insists on neutral ground and won’t visit Moscow, adding friction.  

All sources are recent (late Feb 2026) and from credible outlets (Sky News, RBC-Ukraine, RFE/RL, Interfax). There is no single clear catalyst; the balance of pro- and anti-talk factors leaves substantial uncertainty.

3. MARKET EFFICIENCY ANALYSIS  
A 40–41% probability plausibly reflects a consensus that while Zelenskyy is ready and mediated talks are imminent, Putin’s position is opaque and may remain negative. The narrow bid–ask spread suggests at least one active trader or a small group setting the price. Low volume likely reflects limited interest rather than consensus that the event is essentially settled.

4. MISPRICING ASSESSMENT  
There is no obvious missing data that would swing probability by >10–20 cents. All publicly available statements have been priced in: Zelenskyy’s openness, trilateral efforts in March, and Putin’s reluctance. Absent a clear signal from Putin (e.g., public statement agreeing to a call), moving the market substantially higher or lower would require new intelligence.  

5. EXPECTED VALUE CALCULATION  
Strength of evidence for mispricing: ~30% (some small upside if trilateral talks spin off a bilateral call).  
Potential magnitude of mispricing: ~5 cents.  
Expected value ≈ 30% × 5 cents ≈ 1.5 cents.

EXPECTED_VALUE: 1.5  
SUMMARY: Recent credible reporting shows Zelenskyy eager to talk and trilateral talks in March, but no sign Putin will reciprocate, making a ~40% price plausible. The lack of a strong catalyst suggests any mispricing is small.  
CONFIDENCE: medium
analysis.summary: Recent credible reporting shows Zelenskyy eager to talk and trilateral talks in March, but no sign Putin will reciprocate, making a ~40% price plausible. The lack of a strong catalyst suggests any mispricing is small.

analysis.question: Will Aurora win DreamLeague 28?
analysis.fullAnalysis:
1. CONTEXT ANALYSIS  
The market asks: “Will Aurora win DreamLeague 28?” It resolves YES if Aurora is ultimately the tournament champion by March 1, 2026 (or whenever the final completes). It resolves NO if Aurora is eliminated at any point.  

2. EVIDENCE EVALUATION  
Bearish evidence (suggesting 41% is too high)  
• Group Stage performance: According to GosuGamers, Aurora finished Group Stage 2 with a 4–3 record (3rd place), which put them in the lower bracket of the playoffs—a more perilous route with immediate elimination risk.  
• Strong competition: Team Liquid went 7–0 to top Group 2, and Tundra Esports also look formidable. Aurora’s path in the lower bracket likely pits them against these top teams early, lowering their championship odds.  

Bullish evidence (suggesting 41% is reasonable or low)  
• Historical dominance: Aurora carried a 31-series win streak into DreamLeague and remained unbeaten through February 22 (Hotspawn), indicating elite form and adaptability to the meta.  
• Playoff qualification: Yardbarker confirms Aurora almost scraped in but still made the playoffs, showing resilience.  
• Match-level odds: Luke Warr’s article on Aurora vs. Xtreme Gaming suggests bookmakers give Aurora decent odds in individual series, implying tail potential in best‐of-series contexts.  

Source credibility and recency  
• All sources are dated 2026-02-22 to 2026-02-28, immediately before or as the playoffs begin. They come from specialized esports outlets with up-to-date coverage.  

3. MARKET EFFICIENCY ANALYSIS  
• A 41% probability can be interpreted as a blend of Aurora’s strong baseline skill (perhaps 30–35% implied for a top-4 team) plus some credit for their historic win streak and meta fit.  
• Low volume suggests only a handful of traders have taken positions; they could be fans or those who tracked Aurora’s season-long form. Alternatively, the market may reflect bookie-based odds rather than a diversified informed consensus.  

4. MISPRICING ASSESSMENT  
• There’s no direct model mapping their 4–3 group stage finish and lower-bracket path to a win probability.  
• The tough bracket draw argues for a championship probability below 41%. But Aurora’s streak and match-level odds argue for 30–40%.  
• The overlapping bullish and bearish factors roughly offset, implying the market price sits in a plausible range.  
• No clear >20¢ mispricing emerges—any error likely small (<10¢).  

5. EXPECTED VALUE CALCULATION  
• Strength of evidence: ~20% (we lack a precise win-probability model, and tournament flow can change rapidly).  
• Magnitude of mispricing: ~5¢ (if Aurora’s true probability is ~35% rather than 41%).  
• Expected value = 20% × 5¢ = 1¢.  

EXPECTED_VALUE: 1.0  
SUMMARY: Aurora sits in the lower bracket after a 4–3 group stage and faces stiff competition from Team Liquid and Tundra, arguing for sub-41% win odds, but their historic dominance and match-level strength suggest the market price is in the right vicinity, with any mispricing likely small.  
CONFIDENCE: low

0x3e4e5225054ad5168e0b249164f643453ba97a.question: Ukraine recognizes Russian sovereignty over its territory by December 31, 2026?

analysis.question: Ukraine recognizes Russian sovereignty over its territory by December 31, 2026?
analysis.fullAnalysis:
1. CONTEXT ANALYSIS  
The market asks whether Ukraine will publicly agree—via a mutual deal or unilateral pledge—to formally recognize Russian sovereignty over any UN‐recognized Ukrainian territory by December 31, 2026. “Yes” requires a formal, announced recognition; “No” if no such announcement occurs by the deadline.

2. EVIDENCE EVALUATION  
Bullish (supports higher than 15%):  
- Russia’s negotiating leverage: U.S. appears to push Ukraine to cede territory before security guarantees (ISW, Feb 13).  
- Russia ready to offer back other regions (RBC‐Ukraine, Feb 28). A deal could trade Donbas recognition for returns elsewhere.  

Bearish (supports ≤15%):  
- Zelenskyy’s emphatic public refusal (LIGA.net, Feb 5), reinforced in Axios (Feb 17) by noting public opposition to ceding territory.  
- Consistent Ukrainian government messaging rejects any formal recognition of Russian sovereignty.  
- Russia’s demand for global recognition of Donbas (Kyiv Post, Feb 5) contrasts with Ukraine’s stance, suggesting high diplomatic friction.  

All sources are recent (Feb 2026) and credible (major news outlets, ISW), showing strong, repeated Ukrainian refusal. The pro‐concession evidence (ISW, RBC) shows negotiation pressure but not concrete moves toward formal sovereignty recognition.

3. MARKET EFFICIENCY ANALYSIS  
A 15% price aligns with tail risk: Ukraine might eventually make a bad compromise under U.S. pressure, but strong leadership and public opposition cap that probability. Low volume could simply reflect consensus on low likelihood, not a lone trader.

4. MISPRICING ASSESSMENT  
No clear missing information: public and leadership resolve against recognition seems well known. The small chance of a diplomatic deal trading Donbas recognition for other concessions plausibly justifies a non‐zero probability. Thus, 15% is a reasonable aggregation of tail risk vs. strong political resistance.

5. EXPECTED VALUE CALCULATION  
Strength of evidence missing from price: 10% (market already reflects major factors)  
Magnitude of mispricing if any: 5 cents  
Expected value = 10% × 5 cents = 0.5 cents

EXPECTED_VALUE: 0.5  
SUMMARY: Zelenskyy’s firm refusals and public opposition strongly support a low probability of recognition, while U.S. pressure and Russia’s offer to return other territories keep a small tail risk alive—making 15% a plausible price.  
CONFIDENCE: medium
analysis.summary: Zelenskyy’s firm refusals and public opposition strongly support a low probability of recognition, while U.S. pressure and Russia’s offer to return other territories keep a small tail risk alive—making 15% a plausible price.

0x46ccf5e76fc86d54382165703ce428439ecb4b.question: Trump cabinet member out by March 31, 2026?

analysis.question: Trump cabinet member out by March 31, 2026?
analysis.fullAnalysis:
1. CONTEXT ANALYSIS  
The market asks: “Will any Trump second‐term cabinet appointee leave office by March 31, 2026?” A “Yes” resolves if at least one qualifying cabinet‐level official actually departs before that date; otherwise “No.”  

2. EVIDENCE EVALUATION  
Bullish for “Yes” (>17%)  
• Pam Bondi (Attorney General) has publicly stated odds of removal peaked at 62% and now sit at 54% for departure by the market cutoff.  Because any one departure triggers “Yes,” Bondi’s standalone ~54% exit‐chance implies the “any departure” probability ≥54%.  
• Other insiders’ odds (Kash Patel ~49%, Kristi Noem ~48%) further push the aggregate probability even higher when combined.  
Bearish for “Yes” (<17%)  
• Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick is locked in through 2029, effectively removing one high‐profile name from the list of plausible mid‐term departures.  
• No other cabinet official has publicly signaled intent to leave, and Trump’s first term saw relatively stable senior turnover, tempering overall churn expectations.  
Credibility & Recency  
• The Bondi odds come from a mainstream news‐site report (Daily Beast, Feb 12, 2026) referencing market/bookmaker data. Covers.com (Feb 19) confirms active markets on individual exits. Both are fresh and reasonably reliable for gauging trader sentiment.  

3. MARKET EFFICIENCY ANALYSIS  
• A 17% price implies traders see a very low likelihood any official leaves. That sharply contradicts the documented ~54% single‐member exit odds.  
• The market’s tiny $411 volume and 4¢ spread leave it vulnerable to being set by one or two participants rather than broad arbitrage, especially if most informed players neglect a peripheral question.  
• The outcome is not yet obvious nor heavily scrutinized, suggesting any liquidity providers haven’t priced in the specific Bondi/Patel/Noem odds.  

4. MISPRICING ASSESSMENT  
• Given one member alone (Bondi) has >50% exit odds, the “any departure” probability must exceed 50%, yet the market sits at 17%.  
• There is no plausible read of the resolution criteria that reconciles a 17% market price with a 54% removal chance for a single qualifying official.  
• This discrepancy (≈37¢) is too large to attribute solely to uncertainty about minor cabinet shuffles or small‐skills miscounting; informed traders appear under‐weighting clear, up‐to‐date exit odds for at least one member.  

5. EXPECTED VALUE CALCULATION  
• Strength of evidence: 80% (solid, recent, directly comparable odds data)  
• Magnitude of mispricing: 37¢  
• Expected value ≈ 0.80 × 37¢ = 29.6¢  

EXPECTED_VALUE: 29.6  
SUMMARY: The market price of 17% starkly contrasts with standalone exit odds (e.g., Pam Bondi at ~54%), implying any‐departure odds should be >50%. Low volume suggests inefficiency and a mispricing of roughly 37¢, yielding ~30¢ expected value.  
CONFIDENCE: medium
analysis.summary: The market price of 17% starkly contrasts with standalone exit odds (e.g., Pam Bondi at ~54%), implying any‐departure odds should be >50%. Low volume suggests inefficiency and a mispricing of roughly 37¢, yielding ~30¢ expected value.

analysis.question: Will USD/JPY hit 180 (High) in 2026?
analysis.fullAnalysis:
1. CONTEXT ANALYSIS  
The market asks whether USD/JPY will trade at or above 180.00 on any hourly candle before year-end 2026. A “Yes” requires an hourly high ≥180; otherwise, it resolves “No.”

2. EVIDENCE EVALUATION  
Bearish case (arguing the market is too high):  
• Flux Market (Feb 15 2026): USD/JPY at ~152.7; BOJ hawkishness and Fed pausing cuts suggest limited upside.  
• RBC (Jan 1 2026): Forecast USD/JPY to fall from 156 in Q1 2026 to 135 by Q4 2027.  
• ING (Nov 10 2025): Structural factors point to USD depreciation and weak rebound potential.  
• LSEG (Jan 2026): Fed easing could weaken USD; carry trades at risk if BOJ hikes.  
All four are reputable institutional sources with recent data and technical/ policy analysis pointing away from a 17% rally to 180 this year.

Bullish case (arguing the price is reasonable or understated):  
• Saxo Bank (2022): Highlights risk of yen weakness if BoJ maintains low rates and defends the currency with reserves—but doesn’t project a jump near 180.  
• LSEG: Notes that steep US yield curve can attract investors, potentially supporting USD.  
None forecast or attach significant probability to a move above 180 in 2026.

3. MARKET EFFICIENCY ANALYSIS  
An 18% probability may reflect tail-risk premium for unforeseen shocks (geopolitical crisis, sudden Fed-BoJ divergence). Low volume means one or two traders could be setting the price. If it were a broad consensus, we’d expect more volume at this extreme view or published scenarios assigning tiny odds to 180.

4. MISPRICING ASSESSMENT  
Given near-term trading at 153, strong institutional forecasts for USD weakness, and lack of any published bullish scenarios supporting a 27-point rally, 18% seems too high. A more realistic probability might be in the 5–10% range. I find credible evidence that the “Yes” side is overpriced by roughly 8–13 cents.

5. EXPECTED VALUE CALCULATION  
Strength of evidence: 50% (multiple high-credibility sources but no direct probability models)  
Magnitude of mispricing: 13 cents (18% vs ~5% mid-range fair odds)  
Expected value = 0.50 × 13 cents = 6.5 cents

EXPECTED_VALUE: 6.5  
SUMMARY: Institutional forecasts (Flux, RBC, ING, LSEG) unanimously expect USD/JPY to weaken further, making a rally to 180 in 2026 extremely unlikely. The market’s 18% probability appears overstated, suggesting a mispricing of about 8–13 cents.  
CONFIDENCE: medium

analysis.question: Will CDU win the second most seats in the 2026 Baden-Württemberg parliamentary elections?
analysis.fullAnalysis:
1. CONTEXT ANALYSIS  
This market pays out if the CDU finishes exactly second in seat count in the March 8, 2026 Baden-Württemberg Landtag election. A “YES” outcome requires the CDU to trail exactly one party (most likely the Greens or SPD) but outpace all other parties; if the CDU wins the plurality of seats (i.e. finishes first) or falls to third or lower, the market resolves “NO.”

2. EVIDENCE EVALUATION  
Bullish (+ for YES):  
• A 2/25 Ground.news survey reports the Greens “just behind” the CDU, suggesting the Greens could overtake the CDU and push it into second place.  
• Chancellor Merz’s low approval (Bloomberg 2/20) may weigh on the CDU brand, opening the door for the Greens or SPD to lead in Baden-Württemberg.  

Bearish (+ for NO):  
• INSA (2/23) polling still shows the CDU competitive for the top spot, implying a sizable chance it actually finishes first, in which case “YES” loses.  
• Manuel Hagel’s local lead (The Eye Newspapers 2/21) and traditional CDU strength in rural areas may cement a first-place finish rather than second.  

Source credibility is high for major polling firms (INSA) and reputable outlets (Bloomberg, Wikimedia for historical trends), and all data are from late February 2026—highly recent. However, none provide explicit probabilities of finishing exactly second, only relative ballot-share standings.

3. MARKET EFFICIENCY ANALYSIS  
A 33.9% price roughly aligns with a close CDU–Green race: traders may be inferring roughly a one-in-three probability the CDU cedes first place but still holds second. The low $5.9 K volume suggests limited liquidity, but the tight 5.2¢ spread indicates at least two participants have firm views near this level. It’s likely that informed bettors have already baked in the swing potential from the Greens and Merz’s skepticism, leaving little obvious “edge.”

4. MISPRICING ASSESSMENT  
There’s no single poll giving a stark contradiction to a ~34% chance. Evidence on both sides is balanced: CDU could finish first (negating YES) or slip behind the Greens (affirming YES). Without a clear quantitative expectation unaccounted for by the market, any mispricing seems small (< 10 c). Given the timeline still five days from ballots, uncertainty remains high and the market’s level seems defensible.

5. EXPECTED VALUE CALCULATION  
Strength of evidence that price is mispriced: 30%  
Magnitude of potential mispricing: 5 cents  
Expected value = 0.30 × 5 c = 1.5 c  

EXPECTED_VALUE: 1.5  
SUMMARY: Current polling and recent political dynamics suggest roughly a one-third chance the CDU finishes second, aligning with the 33.9% market price. No strong, overlooked evidence indicates a large mispricing.  
CONFIDENCE: low
analysis.summary: Current polling and recent political dynamics suggest roughly a one-third chance the CDU finishes second, aligning with the 33.9% market price. No strong, overlooked evidence indicates a large mispricing.

0xa2694315da159a18f38a07951f3b3871eadfe5.question: Will the Democratic Party of Korea win 10 or more seats in South Korea’s June 3, 2026 parliamentary by-elections?

analysis.question: Will the Democratic Party of Korea win 10 or more seats in South Korea’s June 3, 2026 parliamentary by-elections?
analysis.fullAnalysis:
1. CONTEXT ANALYSIS  
The market asks: “Will the Democratic Party of Korea (DPK) win 10 or more National Assembly seats in the by-elections held June 3, 2026?”  
– YES resolves if DPK wins at least ten by-election seats on that date.  
– NO if they win nine or fewer, or if results are unknown by year-end (auto‐resolves NO).  
Only seats formally up for by-election on June 3, 2026 count; uncontested seats don’t matter.

2. EVIDENCE EVALUATION  
Bearish (arguing 10+ is extremely unlikely):  
• “Mini-General Election” piece notes only four seats are confirmed vacant; more vacancies require incumbents resigning to run in local polls—an uncertain process. Even if a handful more seats open, reaching ten contests is hard.  
• All research otherwise focuses on local/regional elections (governor mayors, etc.), not parliamentary by-election totals. Strong local poll leads (65% vs 20%) don’t translate to multiplying by-election seats.  
• No source indicates anywhere near ten separate National Assembly vacancies.  

Bullish (supporting >0% chance):  
• High-profile candidates (Song Young-gil, etc.) signal DPK might pick up every available slot.  
• PPP’s candidate shortages and internal strife could create additional vacancies or lower PPP performance.  
• DPK’s strong public sentiment (55% “support government momentum”) could pressure more incumbents to resign and run locally, expanding vacancies.

Credibility & Recency: All articles are from credible Korean outlets in Feb 2026. But their focus is on local elections, not confirming parliamentary vacancy counts.

3. MARKET EFFICIENCY ANALYSIS  
A 17.5% price plausibly reflects a small tail risk that (a) more than four seats open if several MPs step down to run for local posts, and (b) DPK sweeps all of them. Informed traders likely recognize that while the DPK is strong locally, converting that into ten by-election seats requires an unusual level of vacancies. Low volume is probably because most traders see this as near-impossible and haven’t engaged.

4. MISPRICING ASSESSMENT  
There’s no specific credible evidence of mispricing:  
– The improbability of creating ≥10 vacancies is the key constraint; research doesn’t understate that.  
– Local poll strength doesn’t directly imply by-election seat count.  
– The market price seems to capture only the small chance of extraordinary vacancy creation plus a DPK sweep.  

5. EXPECTED VALUE CALCULATION  
Strength of evidence that price is off: 30% (research doesn’t reveal any hidden path to ten seats)  
Magnitude of mispricing if price is off: ~10¢ (best-case misestimation between 17.5% and true ~8%)  
Expected value = 0.30 × 10¢ = 3¢

EXPECTED_VALUE: 3  
SUMMARY: With only four confirmed by-election seats and no clear route to reach ten, the 17.5% probability seems to reflect a small-chance scenario rather than a major mispricing. Local election polling won’t directly translate into parliamentary pickups.  
CONFIDENCE: medium
analysis.summary: With only four confirmed by-election seats and no clear route to reach ten, the 17.5% probability seems to reflect a small-chance scenario rather than a major mispricing. Local election polling won’t directly translate into parliamentary pickups.

analysis.question: Will Russia enter Vozdvyzhivka by March 31?
analysis.fullAnalysis:
1. CONTEXT ANALYSIS  
The market asks whether Russia will control any part of Vozdvyzhivka, Zaporizhzhia Oblast, by March 31, 2026, as shown on the ISW map (or secondary sources if ISW is unavailable). “Yes” requires Russian shading under any relevant layer (infiltration, control, advance, or gains) persisting through a full next daily update cycle. Territorial gains later reversed still count once initially registered. Negotiated de jure control only counts if actual control follows.

2. EVIDENCE EVALUATION  
Bullish Indicators (supporting a higher probability than 35.5%):  
• ISW Feb 27 notes Russia preparing a Spring–Summer 2026 offensive, with ongoing advances near Hulyaipole not far south of Vozdvyzhivka, implying potential spillover.  
• General Staff reports (Feb 9) of 74 attacks in Pokrovsk and Huliaipole directions suggest Russia is concentrating pressure in southern Zaporizhzhia sectors.  
• UNN Jan 31 indicates multiple (25) failed Russian breach attempts near Vozdvyzhivka; high operational tempo hints that one may succeed by late March.

Bearish Indicators (supporting a lower probability):  
• Ukrainian forces are reported holding defensive lines effectively; the UNN Jan 31 piece details repelled assaults and heavy Russian losses (880 personnel, ~700 UAVs in a day).  
• Tregubov’s Jan 30 report focuses pressure further north (Vovchansk sector), suggesting Russia’s main effort may remain elsewhere, limiting resources available to push into Vozdvyzhivka.  
• No reports so far of Russian territory within Vozdvyzhivka itself; forward advances near Hulyaipole don’t guarantee a southern thrust reaching that specific village by late March.

Source Credibility and Recency:  
• ISW and Ukrainian General Staff reports are high credibility, recent (late Jan–Feb).  
• UNN and Ukrinform are reputable Ukrainian outlets; battlefield numbers may be optimistic but consistent with other open-source tracking.

Obviousness Check:  
The evidence of a spring offensive and heavy attacks is fairly well known among open-source analysts. The market’s 35.5% price already reflects a nontrivial chance based on that trend. There is no single overlooked report that dramatically shifts the balance.

3. MARKET EFFICIENCY ANALYSIS  
• A 35.5% price plausibly synthesizes Ukraine’s resilient defense (holding lines) with Russia’s Spring campaign preparations and localized pressure.  
• Low volume could reflect that most traders view this as a niche tactical question rather than a high-stakes strategic pivot, leaving price formation to a few informed participants rather than broad liquidity.  
• The tight spread (5¢) suggests that the few participants who do trade are confident in their private estimates, not that the market is deeply liquid.

4. MISPRICING ASSESSMENT  
Given balanced indicators—stout Ukrainian defenses vs. mounting Russian offensive capacity—and no clear “smoking gun” suggesting an imminent breakthrough or collapse, the 35.5% price sits within a reasonable range. No recent source provides a decisive tilt >20¢ away from current pricing. The timeline (one month to go) still carries typical battlefield uncertainties, which appear factored in.

5. EXPECTED VALUE CALCULATION  
Strength of evidence that the market is mispriced: ~10% (no strong missing data)  
Magnitude of mispricing if wrong: ~0–5¢ (small)  
Expected value ≈ 10% × 5¢ = 0.5¢ (rounded to 1¢ or effectively negligible)

EXPECTED_VALUE: 1  
SUMMARY: Open-source reports show both robust Ukrainian defenses around Vozdvyzhivka and credible signs of a Russian spring offensive, which seems priced into a 35.5% probability. No clear missing evidence suggests a material mispricing.  
CONFIDENCE: medium

0x49ddcacf6c172c59598a6dec8e5eff90d971cb.question: Will annual inflation increase by 2.5% in March?

analysis.question: Will annual inflation increase by 2.5% in March?
analysis.fullAnalysis:
1. CONTEXT ANALYSIS  
This market asks whether the BLS will report a year-over-year CPI increase of at least 2.5% for March 2026 (unadjusted, to one decimal). A “YES” requires CPI₍Mar 2026₎ ≥ 2.5%; a “NO” is CPI < 2.5%.  

2. EVIDENCE EVALUATION  
Bullish signals (supporting a move ≥2.5%):  
• January PPI jumped 0.5% month-over-month (Reuters, USA Today), suggesting cost pass-through to consumers with a 2–3-month lag.  
• Fading favorable base effects may lift the YOY CPI reading when comparing March 2026 vs. March 2025’s relatively low readings.  

Bearish signals (supporting <2.5%):  
• FactSet projects January CPI at 2.4% YOY; February likely similar, leaving only a small 0.1-point jump needed in March.  
• Finflowmax highlights cooling core CPI at 2.4% and divergent forecasts; absent a clear demand shock or policy shift, a sustained spike is uncertain.  

All sources are late-Feb 2026 or earlier, from reputable outlets (Reuters, FactSet). None point to a near-certain ≥2.5% CPI for March—most see inflation broadly stable around 2.4%. If a >0.1% jump were likely, it would already be reflected in higher probabilities; the 15.5% price seems to incorporate the modest upside risk.  

3. MARKET EFFICIENCY ANALYSIS  
A 15.5% probability plausibly reflects consensus: CPI has hovered around 2.4%, with only a low-probability driver (tariff passthrough, energy spikes) capable of pushing it to ≥2.5%. Low volume suggests few traders are wagering on a marginal 0.1-point swing, not necessarily that the price is wildly off.  

4. MISPRICING ASSESSMENT  
There is no clear, high-confidence evidence that an upside surprise to ≥2.5% is being overlooked. The modest risk factors (PPI pass-through, fading base effects) are already slim and likely priced in. A significant mispricing (>20 cents) would require a near-certain shock, which no credible source identifies.  

5. EXPECTED VALUE CALCULATION  
Strength of evidence for mispricing: ~40% (some upside risk but already incorporated)  
Magnitude of mispricing: ~5 cents (max plausible undervaluation)  
Expected value = 40% × 5 cents = 2 cents  

EXPECTED_VALUE: 2.0  
SUMMARY: Current forecasts cluster around 2.4% CPI in early 2026, with only modest upside risk from PPI pass-through and fading base effects. There’s no clear, high-confidence signal that a ≥2.5% print is underpriced.  
CONFIDENCE: medium
